hq: Tarrytown, New York, USA (Regeneron contact page: https://www.regeneron.com/contact)
aging_relevance_reasoning: Regeneron’s marketed portfolio includes therapies directly used in age-associated conditions and biology-adjacent pathways: (1) EYLEA (aflibercept) is indicated for neovascular (wet) age-related macular degeneration (nAMD), and AMD is an age-related eye disease (National Eye Institute: https://www.nei.nih.gov/learn-about-eye-health/eye-conditions-and-diseases/age-related-macular-degeneration). (2) Multiple marketed biologics modulate inflammatory cytokine signaling (e.g., Dupixent for type 2 inflammation and Kevzara targeting the IL-6 receptor; product pages: https://www.regeneron.com/products/dupixent and https://www.regeneron.com/products/kevzara), which overlaps mechanistically with inflammation-related aging hallmarks (analyst mapping). (3) Regeneron also highlights large-scale human genetics for target discovery via the Regeneron Genetics Center, which can be relevant for identifying targets associated with healthspan-related traits (analyst interpretation).

existing_longevity_assets.indication: Neovascular (wet) age-related macular degeneration (nAMD) (and other retinal diseases listed on product page)
existing_longevity_assets.relevance: Treats an explicitly age-related condition (AMD), relevant to maintaining visual function with aging; mechanistically acts on VEGF signaling (age-related tissue microenvironment/communication; analyst mapping).

existing_longevity_assets.indication: Type 2 inflammatory diseases including atopic dermatitis and asthma (see product page for full list of approved uses)
existing_longevity_assets.relevance: Anti-inflammatory cytokine-pathway modulation (IL-4/IL-13 pathway per product description), mapping to chronic inflammation / altered intercellular communication hallmarks (analyst mapping).
Targets IL-6 receptor signaling (per product description), relevant to systemic inflammatory signaling that overlaps with inflammaging-related biology (analyst mapping).

existing_longevity_assets.indication: Recurrent pericarditis and certain rare autoinflammatory conditions (see product page for full list of approved uses)
existing_longevity_assets.relevance: IL-1 pathway inhibition (per product description) is a direct anti-inflammatory mechanism that overlaps with chronic inflammation hallmark (analyst mapping).

Addresses severe lifelong dyslipidemia; lipid biology is relevant to cardiometabolic risk trajectories that commonly manifest with aging, though HoFH itself is a genetic condition (analyst framing of aging relevance).

existing_longevity_assets.indication: Multiple cancer indications including cutaneous squamous cell carcinoma and others (see product page for full list of approved uses)
existing_longevity_assets.relevance: Immune checkpoint modulation (per product description) is relevant to immune function in older populations, though primarily pursued for oncology rather than aging biology (analyst framing).

recent_acquisitions.relevance: Potential oncology pipeline expansion; any aging/longevity relevance would be indirect via cancer burden in older adults.
Hearing loss interventions can be relevant to healthy aging; source needed for deal specifics and positioning.
